name: Dakota Buffalo & Beer Pie
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Commercially raised buffalo, cut into 1 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the meat cubes with salt, pepper, and sage, and dredge in 1/4 cup of flour.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet and brown the meat on all sides using a slotted spoon to transfer it to a heavy Dutch oven.
3. In the remaining oil in the skillet, sauté onion, carrot, celery, and potato until lightly browned, then add vegetables to the meat in the Dutch oven.
4. Sprinkle the remaining flour over drippings in the skillet and cook, stirring until lightly browned.
5. Stir in the broth, tomato puree, beer, garlic, bay leaf, parsley, clove, and thyme.
6. Pour the mixture over the meat and vegetables. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer until the meat is tender (about 1-1/2 hours).
7. Pour into a deep 9 inch pie dish and let cool. When the meat is cool, roll out pastry and cover the dish.
8. Cut steam vents in the crust and bake in a preheated 425F oven for 30-35 minutes, until the pastry is browned. Makes 6 servings.
